subject: Ecology
title: Mangrove Forests
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Mangrove Forests.

hook.text: Mangroves grow where no other tree can—rooted in salty coastal mud, flooded by tides.

buildup.text: Their tangled roots filter sediment, protect coasts from storms, and trap carbon rapidly.

discovery.text: Young fish, shrimp, and crabs use mangrove roots as nurseries safe from open-water predators.

twist.text: Mangroves store up to four times more carbon per hectare than tropical rainforests.

climax.text: Despite their value, over a third of the world's mangroves have been destroyed since 1980.

quiz.question: Why are mangrove roots important for young fish?
